ogTitle: Horningsea Park vs Hornsby Heights: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Horningsea Park and Hornsby Heights.
heading: Horningsea Park vs Hornsby Heights — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Horningsea Park, New South Wales and Hornsby Heights, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hornsby Heights has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Horningsea Park (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Horningsea Park or Hornsby Heights safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Horningsea Park has a flood risk score of 38/100, while Hornsby Heights scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Horningsea Park scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Hornsby Heights scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Horningsea Park or Hornsby Heights?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hornsby Heights has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
